name: La Peche Chocolate Velvet
recipeIngredient: Creme fraiche or whipped cream: optional
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a 2 1/2 qt souffle dish with plastic wrap.
2. Cut the pound cake into thin slices and line the bottom and sides of the souffle dish with overlapping pieces, ensuring no openings. Place 2 layers of pound cake at the bottom to prevent leaks. Sprinkle 2 tbsp of amaretto around the pound cake. Set aside.
3. To prepare the mousse: In a bowl, combine 1/3 cup amaretto, rum, chopped Heath bars, melted butter, egg yolks, and coffee. Stir well.
4. Melt 1 1/2 lbs of semisweet chocolate in a double boiler. Allow it to cool slightly. Whip the cream until stiff; set aside. With clean beaters, whip the egg whites with a pinch of salt until stiff. Gradually add 1 tbsp powdered sugar and beat for 1 minute.
5. Slowly incorporate the chocolate into the liquor mixture, stirring well to combine evenly. Gently fold in the whipped cream and egg whites. Pour into the prepared souffle dish.
6. Cover the mousse with remaining pound cake slices, overlapping as needed. Return plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 10 hours.
7. To make the icing: Melt 1/2 lb semisweet chocolate. Using an electric mixer, beat in powdered sugar until smooth. Add coffee to achieve desired consistency (similar to buttercream). Let cool slightly before spreading.
8. To serve: Invert the cake onto a serving plate and remove plastic wrap. Spread icing over the top. Refrigerate. Cut into slices and serve with creme fraiche or whipped cream, if desired. Create chocolate leaves by tempering chocolate and garnishing the velvet.
9. Store refrigerated up to 10 days or freeze for up to six months.
